linux-gfx90a.blocked_reason: Session 5 (fork 2f60595 on top of d326818; cuco fork 0fb53f8): MAJOR PROGRESS -- libcugraph.so now LINKS (all 16 remaining SG library TUs compile) and PAGERANK_TEST passes 56/56 on real gfx90a vs the CPU reference (rmat + karate/dolphins files, incl edge-masking, weighted, personalized). The 8 last primitives cleared via mechanical USE_HIP tuple-flavor fixes (proclaim_return_type exact-match, kv_store is_equivalent_value_type asserts, templated sampling-functor key params, double->int static_cast, warp_size_ct constexpr, single-type hipCUB SortPairs offset functor) plus a thrust/tuple.h converting CONSTRUCTOR from cuda::std::tuple (reverse of the session-4 conversion operator) and classic-vs-cuda::std operator<. Test harness adapted: base_fixture.hpp to the ported rmm's older MR API (no any_resource), tests CMake wired with raft/thrust compat shims + host warp-size, tellg fpos->streamoff. cuco fork: gated the 3 pre-Volta __CUDA_ARCH__<700 guards on defined(__CUDA_ARCH__) (HIP-undefined macro folded to true and misapplied the >8B-slot / cas_dependent_write restriction). Also FIXED a real wave64 GPU fault in transform_e_packed_bool: the packed-bool ballot used the full 64-lane wave mask but a wavefront spans two words with different loop bounds, so a small graph splits the wave mid-loop and the divergent full-wave __ballot_sync faults; now ballots only the lane's own 32-lane group (arch-unified wave32/64). All USE_HIP-guarded, CUDA byte-identical. REMAINING (why still blocked, no false-port): BFS_TEST and SSSP_TEST build and run but compute WRONG distances on the rmat graph (rmat_small_test correctness fails 'distances do not match'); PageRank on the same infra is exact, isolating the bug to the vertex-frontier expansion path (transform_reduce_if_v_frontier_outgoing_e_by_dst + per_v_transform_reduce_if_incoming_outgoing_e + frontier buckets; no raw wave intrinsics -- lowers to rocPRIM BlockScan/DeviceSelect/reduce_by_key, so bisect that on the deterministic rmat seed-0 graph next session). This BFS/SSSP traversal bug was first observable only now that the .so links. notes.md Session 5 has exact sites.
